revisedPrompt:
Generate exactly one PNG image: a 64x64 pixel-art terrain-tile sheet for a 16-bit SNES-style platformer/RPG world-map forest mass tileset.

Canvas and grid:
- Exact final sheet size: 64x64 px.
- 4 rows x 4 columns, 16 frames total.
- Each frame is exactly 16x16 px.
- No visible grid lines, no borders, no gaps, no tile IDs, no labels, no watermark, no UI.
- Every frame must stay fully inside its own 16x16 cell and keep consistent scale and identity.
- Cells fill edge-to-edge with tile art; any empty/background pixels must be solid #FF00FF magenta only.

Subject:
Top-down transition TILESET of a dense bright mythical-Japan satoyama forest canopy mass seen directly from above. It is a geographic forest region, not individual tree sprites. Use layered broadleaf treetop masses inspired by camphor and chinquapin: rounded clustered canopy shapes, deep saturated early-summer greens, sunlit leaf highlights, and darker shadowed interior clusters. Canopy edges meet open vivid green grass naturally with organic breakup.

Strict 4x4 layout:
Row 1: four full CENTER variations, dense canopy filling the entire tile.
Row 2: edge tiles with neighbour open grass terrain on the North side, South side, West side, and East side, in that order. Forest edge should organically meet grass.
Row 3: outer corner transition tiles NW, NE, SW, SE, in that order. Canopy corner shapes should leave matching open grass at the outside corner.
Row 4: four accent CENTER variations: darker interior center, shadowed edge-feel center, subtle road or path cut-through within forest mass, and satoyama accent center with tiny vermilion/white shrine-like color flecks embedded in canopy without forming a character, logo, letter, building, or icon.

Tilemap quality:
- Seamlessly tileable tiles, cohesive palette and lighting.
- Avoid square stamp repetition; vary texture, canopy silhouette, edge breakup, and highlights.
- Final game view must not read as a checkerboard.
- Terrain transitions blend grass and canopy naturally, not hard rectangular bands.
- Include center, edge, outer corner, darker interior, shadowed variant, grass-overlap, chipped/irregular edge, road cut-through, and small decorative accent variants through the requested layout.

Visual style:
- Crisp low-resolution pixel art, no antialiasing blur, no painterly smoothing.
- 16-bit Japanese RPG world-map style, bright cheerful colors.
- Bright mythical-Japan satoyama palette: deep rich saturated greens like early-summer rice-field banks, clear blue-green water-tinted shadow greens, small vermilion and white accents, soft warm sunlight.
- NOT dry, NOT yellow-brown, NOT a western prairie or wasteland.
- Top-left light source.
- Fine readable pixel clusters, consistent palette.
- Dark one-pixel outline or dark leaf-cluster edging where useful.

Avoid completely:
No Mario, Nintendo, Zelda, Final Fantasy, Pokemon, Dragon Quest, Secret of Mana, Okami, Sakuna characters, logos, likenesses, or references. No samurai armor, kabuto helmet, ninja outfit, two-sword daisho, gun, modern objects, horror mood, dark gloomy palette, bird, companion creature, letter emblems, modern text, watermark, or UI.
